nodes.label: AI as quick answer provider
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for detailed or nuanced information
nodes.grounding_span: unless I need to be 100% of detailed or nuanced information, a quick ai response to a Google search usually has the answer i'm looking for

nodes.label: AI reliability and factuality
nodes.grounding_span: for some things, the ai response may not be factually accurate, so if it's important i'll be sure to find sources to confirm

nodes.label: AI as collaborator for iterative refinement
nodes.label_negative: AI as one-shot independent task completer
nodes.grounding_span: definitely collaborate. the first response is not generally precisely what I need

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for core skills
nodes.grounding_span: AI just makes me more efficient

nodes.label: Human judgment in customer interactions
nodes.grounding_span: I definitely prefer to handle customer interactions myself, as I'm not sure if I trust ai enough to take the right tone

nodes.label: AI reliance vs. maintaining independent thinking
nodes.grounding_span: she is not choosing to think on her own about projects very much at all

nodes.label: Accuracy and reliability

nodes.label: Effective communication and customer satisfaction
nodes.grounding_span: work together with the customer for the solution

nodes.grounding_span: I still need to know how to write and how to communicate
